summary: DHH recounts nearly buying the Staind album based on the strength of two MTV singles, 'It's Been A While' and 'Outside.' He caught himself by remembering past disappointments from albums bought on singles alone. He instead found a friend with a digital copy and confirmed his suspicion: the album is overwhelmingly heavy metal filler that betrays the promise of its radio-friendly tracks.
key_points: The two Staind singles on MTV rotation are genuinely appealing with their dry vocals and guitar work
Past experience with disappointing album purchases based on singles prevented an impulse buy
The full album reveals a heavy metal direction that clashes with the accessible sound of the singles
File-sharing services circa 2002 were too unreliable for full albums, so borrowing from friends was the pragmatic move
money_quotes: The singles trap almost had me. But I side-stepped it.
It's a sheep in wolves clothing!
Life is too short to scour file-sharing services for full albums in decent quality.
Got the full album down in no time. Got majorly disappointet in no time.
Most of the tracks takes Heavy Metal to a whole new scary place.
key_insight: Try before you buy — the best singles on an album can be misleading advertisements for a deeply disappointing whole.
